sanskrit: यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव । न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥
hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन  लोग जिसको संन्यास कहते हैं, उसीको तुम योग समझो क्योंकि संकल्पोंका त्याग किये बिना मनुष्य कोईसा भी योगी नहीं हो सकता ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna, what people call renunciation is yoga. No one becomes a yogi without giving up intentions.
essence: Yoga begins when the mind stops clutching its own agenda.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Real renunciation is not outer withdrawal. It is letting go of inner plans and fixed desires so the mind can truly enter yoga.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing that inner freedom matters more than outward form.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen while two armies wait. Krishna clarifies that true renunciation is not merely leaving action behind; it is dropping mental intentions. Without that inner release, no one can truly live yoga.
modernRelevance: You close the laptop, but your mind keeps rehearsing the outcome of the conversation. The real letting go is not in your hands or schedule — it is in the plans you keep gripping.
translationLiteral: यं (what) / संन्यासम् (renunciation) / इति (thus) / प्राहुः (they call) / योगम् (yoga) / तम् (that) / विद्धि (know) / पाण्डव (Arjuna) / न (not) / हि (indeed) / असंन्यस्त (not given up) / सङ्कल्पः (intention) / योगी (yogi) / भवति (becomes) / कश्चन (anyone)
